claim: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) removes humans from the learning loop by automating reward signal verification
actionable: When designing RL systems, define deterministic reward functions that can automatically evaluate agent behavior without manual feedback

claim: LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) can reduce memory usage by 60% while retaining good accuracy when fine-tuning models
actionable: Use LoRA for efficient fine-tuning on consumer hardware; add only 1-5% extra weights to the model rather than full parameter updates

claim: Reinforcement learning can be run locally on consumer RTX GPUs; previously it required million-dollar specialized machines
actionable: Set up local RL training on your gaming PC using tools like Unsloth, WSL, and CUDA; this enables custom model training at home

claim: The GRPO training algorithm can teach models to master complex tasks like games through iterative strategy refinement and reward feedback
actionable: Use GRPO for task-specific model training; implement reward functions that capture success criteria and iterate until performance plateaus

claim: Models can generate code (via prompts) that can fail, but these failures provide valuable learning signal in RLVR workflows
actionable: In RLVR, allow model-generated code to fail gracefully and feed failure signals back as negative rewards; don't filter out all failures
